Andy Stone Get in touch

Strategic Pitch | Tanium

From digital brochure to AI-driven demand engine.

Tanium has great product-market fit — proven by industry reports and customer case studies. What's important now is building a modern digital demand strategy that can grow the company toward IPO.

Role

Modern Marketing Leader

Focus

First Touch to SQL

Introduction

I've spent nearly two decades helping companies grow through design programs, digital strategy initiatives, and integrated marketing campaigns. When looking at any new company, I start with research to understand the baseline so there's a shared understanding of how we can grow from here.

Tanium needs an AI-native marketing leader who can help drive the go-to-market strategy and create awareness, drive qualified leads, and turn those into direct sales opportunities. Outside of one-off campaigns and responding quickly to external market factors, the most important thing is to have a repeatable demand engine that covers programs, channels, processes, and underlying technologies to consistently build pipeline. And the team needs to do all of it working with agents to move faster and more effectively than the competition.

I'm coming at this from an outsider's perspective, so some recommendations may already be in flight or there may be internal reasons for taking a different route. This site is meant only for internal teams at Tanium to reference and to start conversations.

Thank you,
Andy

Download Resume →

Recognition for my current work at NetApp

"We ranked NetApp's homepage the highest overall and specifically in branding because it offers an authentic experience."

— Steve Ohanians, Forbes · What B2B Companies Can Learn About UX From Fortune 500 Websites

Strategy

Modern Digital Demand

I've been looking at Tanium for the last couple of months and saw the rapid change this week with a more dynamic homepage focusing directly on the messaging around Mythos and the existential threats it poses for the industry. This is a critical opportunity for Tanium to become the authoritative voice within the market, and to have the entire industry understand that Tanium is ready for this new world of agent-driven vulnerabilities and exploits. It also looks like Claude was used to help create the response quickly, and that's a critical step towards being a market leader that can compete in the current landscape.

With a focused team of marketers who are enabled and encouraged to move fast and use the most modern tools, Tanium is able to look at its entire marketing funnel, making improvements from first awareness to sales handoffs. My background would recommend starting at the top of the funnel because it allows for more possibilities going into every test and idea down the road, and is crucial in today's winner-take-all world for SaaS companies.

Tanium's largest asset in marketing is the primary corporate website. Every customer, partner, visitor and employee interacts with it, and the data coming out of it can inform a year's worth of work and refinements. While it is a large undertaking to evaluate every step of the process, it is the biggest opportunity for growth in the coming year. The funnel below calls out individual actions at each stage that could be made better on the site to increase SQLs.

Tanium's Funnel with High Priority Opportunities

Awareness

SEO · Paid · Social · GenAI/AI Search · 3rd Party Validation

Interest

A/B Testing · Offering Next Steps · Customers Answering Questions

Consideration

Demos · ROI Calculators · On-Site AI Powered Chat · Simplified Forms

Intent

In-Person Demos · Webinars · Events · Engineering Showcase

Purchase

Working Hand in Hand with Sales

Every one of these stages in the funnel is critical to the ongoing success of the company. Marketing, product and sales need to be aligned here to understand the current gaps, fill in prioritization needs, and look at funding requirements to tackle the problems. For example: Tanium's current leadership in industry reports like Gartner is a major advantage for the company, and it feels like a straightforward opportunity to capitalize on that success and capture more of the market in the coming year.

With the amount of changes needed, and the amount of work that needs to be done, the entire team needs to be encouraged and enabled to utilize the most modern AI tools to research, create, and automate work. As Jensen Huang says: bringing in AI isn't about replacing employees unless executives don't have any more ideas that they want to work on.

Perspectives on AI

Every single contributor in a marketing or sales organization should be using AI to speed up their workflows, create more efficiently, and discover new ways of approaching problems.

In all of these ways, I see AI as a supercharger for teams, not a replacement.

This site was created quickly with the assistance of various AI tools because I thought it would be an interesting exercise for this application. I used Perplexity for public research, SEMRush for technical and AI search research, Claude Code to create the overall page, and Figma MCP to send assets directly into the layouts. The writing was done by hand because I needed to synthesize too many disparate sources, and attempts at AI drafting were taking longer to edit than was worth it for a one-off project like this.

Agentic Team Model

Strategy for humans.
Work for agents.

The biggest shift in how I'd structure this team isn't the headcount — it's the architecture. Agents aren't tools that sit inside someone's workflow. They're team members with defined responsibilities, human oversight, and accountable output.

01 AI Agent

Signal & Research

Monitors industry news, competitor moves, analyst reports, and AI security events in real time. When something relevant breaks, it delivers a brief to the Content Strategist within the hour. No human is manually watching feeds.

02 AI Agent

Content Draft

Takes a brief and produces first drafts: blog posts, email sequences, landing page copy, ad variants. A human edits and approves before anything publishes. Collapses draft-to-publish from days to hours.

03 AI Agent

SEO & GEO Reporting

Tracks keyword rankings and AI search presence daily, identifies content gaps, and flags when a competitor outranks Tanium on a high-intent query. Generates content briefs automatically for every cluster that needs to exist — including those in AI-generated answers and summaries.

04 AI Agent

Paid Media

Runs A/B tests on ad creative within guardrails set by the Growth Manager, adjusts bids based on performance signals, and surfaces anomalies for human review. The human sets strategy and budget; the agent handles the optimization loop that used to require daily manual attention.

05 AI Agent

Nurture & CRM

Watches site behavior and triggers personalized sequences based on engagement signals — what pages someone read, what they downloaded, whether they've been here before. SDR alerts and nurture tracks run automatically without manual campaign setup for each play.

06 AI Agent

Ongoing Reporting

Tracks every stage of the marketing funnel, looking for areas of growth or opportunities. Ready to be queried by anyone within the company to ask for reports so that no one is waiting on MBRs or QBRs to get a full picture.

Humans Own

  • — Strategy, brand judgment, and executive relationships
  • — Budget decisions and prioritization
  • — Anything that requires accountability
  • — High impact and high touch opportunities

Agents Own

  • — Execution volume and signal monitoring
  • — First drafts and optimization loops
  • — The speed layer that makes fast-response programs possible
  • — Marketing development and front-end work

The goal isn't to replace the team. It's to make three humans do the work of eight — and let the senior people focus entirely on the decisions that actually move the business.

Technical Baseline

An audit of the existing site utilizing publicly-available information shows a number of opportunities for Tanium's website.

3.1s

Time to First Byte on key listing pages. The industry benchmark is under 500ms.

25

Lighthouse score for the blog index, which is the lowest on the site

7

Mandatory form fields required from cold traffic to request a demo or unlock useful content across the site

0

Active personalization paths being delivered on the Tanium homepage for visitors on the site

Performance Audit

Lighthouse mobile scores. Green is 90+. Orange is 50–89. Red is below 50.

Page
Performance
Accessibility
Best Practices
SEO
Homepage
42
90
96
92
Content Hub
42
90

96

90
Issues
100
Blog Post
50
89
81
100

Source: Lighthouse Mobile audit, 2026. No page clears the performance threshold. The Blog Index has a Best Practices fail.

While the site has some issues with the performance scores, these are easily fixable with the correct focus and prioritization for the teams.

Quick Hits

From auditing the current site today, there are a number of tactical changes that could help meaningfully improve the demand funnel before any shift in overall strategy.

01

Organic traffic is declining

Per SimilarWeb, tanium.com gets ~267K visits/month — but traffic dropped 12.4%. This is likely due in part to the rise of GenAI/AI search, with more research being done inside chat agents. Tanium needs to solidify its GenAI/AI search strategy to account for the shift away from traditional organic search traffic.

02

Personalization is missing in action

A CISO at a Fortune 100 bank and an IT admin at a mid-market retailer currently see the same homepage. No persona-based content paths, no industry-specific landing pages, no ABM-personalized experiences. 6sense is already in the stack — it could be used immediately.

03

The site is managed with older technology

Competitors and startups are realizing that they can make marketing pages and tests within minutes or hours using Claude Code and an empowered team. Let the team loose with these new tools and encourage exploration and play to make things that are bold and new.

04

The demo form is heavy for cold traffic

The "See a Demo" form requires seven fields on first touch — company email, first and last name, job title, phone, company name, and country — with no lighter conversion path or progressive profiling option. This creates unnecessary friction at the most critical conversion moment.

05

Events calendar needs more owned events

Currently seeing 7 upcoming events listed — almost all third-party trade shows. The calendar needs more webinars and demo opportunities that prospective clients can easily join. Fewer form fields on these would help too.

06

Interactive conversion tools are absent

There aren't many places for prospects to explore on their own terms. ROI calculators, interactive product tours, and self-guided demos would help. Competitors like CrowdStrike are moving toward these to warm up leads earlier in the journey.

07

No visible nurture architecture

The site doesn't give visitors many next steps. When visitors finish an article or page, there should be "if you read this, here's what's next" content flows, tracked and used to fuel progressive profiling and further personalization.

08

JavaScript dependency breaks the site

The site fails entirely when JavaScript is disabled — a real problem for bot crawlers and accessibility. Delivering pages as static assets would fix this and meaningfully speed up the site for everyone.

Current popups require so many forms to unlock content that Tanium wants visitors to consume now

Every visitor sees the same examples, case studies and solutions without personalization

A Test from My Time at NetApp

Asking Questions

One small but important test I ran with my team was to simply ask questions to our customers on the site. It started as a small test on one page but grew into whole-site profiling efforts. The assumption: customers in the B2B space don't want to waste time, and will tell you what they're looking for if you make it easy. So we just asked: what do you want?

Tanium could benefit from a similar strategy — profiling the customer throughout the process and offering relevant information at every stage. Simply ask: What industry are you in? What are you looking to secure? What issues have you had in the past? It makes the site feel more human, and speeds up the entire sales process.

Thank you for the opportunity

This is an introductory look at how Tanium could transform its marketing into more predictable, digitally oriented growth.

Let's talk → Download Resume →

My Time at NetApp

Led a full transformation of NetApp's global demand engine — a 20,000-page site across 10 languages. In the first year after relaunch, we doubled organic traffic, increased SQLs by 11%, and earned Forbes' #1 ranking in B2B digital experience.

Founded & Acquired

Founded Emerson Stone, a B2B design and strategy agency (clients: IBM, 3M, Wells Fargo, Twitter) — acquired by Sawaya Consulting. Before that, co-founded Mocavo, a genealogical search engine that grew to the third largest in the industry before acquisition by FindMyPast.

How I Work

I learn from sales and marketing teams first — to find out what's working, where investment is needed, and how to prioritize. I'm drawn to fast-moving environments where decisions are data-driven and where I can be useful to many teams, not just my own.
